title: agropyron repens
intro: Agropyron repens, also known as creeping bentgrass or false brome, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. Agropyron repens is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use is largely anecdotal and more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
